name: TACO: Trash Annotations in Context
annotation_format: COCO instance segmentation; can derive boxes from segmentation/bbox fields
strength: Real-world litter scenes, hierarchical waste taxonomy, masks and boxes
risk: No official train/val/test split; many rare classes; needs COCO-to-YOLO/class-crop conversion
action: Download official annotations/images, run COCO adapter, map 60 classes to project classes and/or TACO-10

name: MJU-Waste v1.0
annotation_format: PASCAL VOC; COCO instance annotations also available
strength: Waste object segmentation with RGB-D imagery; official repository and academic citation
risk: Mostly waste-vs-background, not fine-grained material classes; Google Drive download/manual access
action: Use for object-localization/segmentation evidence and feature distribution, not final multi-class sorting alone

name: ZeroWaste
annotation_format: Detection/semantic segmentation in cluttered industrial waste scenes
strength: In-the-wild industrial-grade clutter, useful for real-world robustness
risk: Different domain from household/public litter; download page must be verified before use
action: Treat as external-domain stress test, not direct class-balance replacement

name: TrashCan 1.0
annotation_format: Instance segmentation masks; material and instance label versions
strength: Real-world annotated debris with masks; useful for detection/segmentation discussion
risk: Underwater domain is very different from phone-camera household waste; academic/research-use license
action: Use as 'similar annotated debris dataset' in comparison table; avoid merging into final household sorting unless domain is justified

name: TrashNet
strength: Classic 6-class waste classification baseline; easy to train ANN/CNN/classical features
risk: Clean/staged backgrounds, not TACO-like detection; cannot directly train detector without synthetic/full-image boxes
action: Use for classification baseline only and explicitly mark as clean-background comparison

name: Garbage in Images (GINI)
annotation_format: Bounding boxes for one trash class
strength: Real-world garbage in images; useful for background/negative context and one-class detection
risk: Single class; not enough for material sorting
action: EDA/features only or binary trash-vs-background; do not use as standalone multi-class model
